title: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture
rawPassageText:
READING PASSAGE 1
You should spend about 20 minutes on Questions 1-13, which are based on Reading Passage 1 below.

The Future of Green Architecture

A

Green architecture was once associated mainly with unusual houses covered in plants or experimental buildings designed for magazines. By 2026, it has moved much closer to the centre of ordinary construction. Cities are under pressure to reduce carbon emissions, protect residents from heat and use less water. Buildings matter because they consume energy during construction and throughout their lives. Architects therefore increasingly describe a good building not only as one that looks attractive, but as one that uses resources carefully, keeps people comfortable and can adapt as the climate changes.

B

The first generation of green buildings often focused on visible features such as solar panels, rooftop gardens and walls of glass. These can still be useful, but designers now argue that the future depends just as much on less obvious decisions. A building's orientation may reduce the need for artificial cooling. Shading can stop summer heat before it enters a room. Natural ventilation can move air through corridors and stairwells, while insulation can keep indoor temperatures stable. In many climates, these passive design choices reduce energy demand before expensive technology is added.

C

Materials are another important part of the shift. Concrete and steel remain common, but their production creates large amounts of carbon dioxide. For this reason, some architects are using engineered timber, recycled bricks, low-carbon concrete and reused steel from demolished buildings. The aim is not to pretend that every material is perfect. Timber must come from well-managed forests, and recycled materials still need testing. However, choosing materials with lower embodied carbon can reduce the environmental cost of a building before anyone switches on a light or an air conditioner.

D

Water is becoming a design issue as well as an engineering issue. Some new buildings collect rainwater from roofs and store it for toilets, cleaning or gardens. Others treat greywater from sinks and showers so that it can be used again. In dry cities, architects are also replacing lawns with local plants that survive with little irrigation. These decisions may not be as dramatic as a tower covered in greenery, but they can make a building much less dependent on mains water. As droughts become more frequent, water-saving design is likely to move from a desirable extra to a basic requirement.

E

Green architecture is not only about the environment outside a building. Designers are paying more attention to the health of people inside it. Daylight can reduce the need for electric lighting and may improve concentration. Better ventilation can remove pollutants that build up indoors. Some offices now use sensors to monitor temperature, humidity and air quality, although designers warn that technology should not replace good planning. A building that saves energy but feels dark, noisy or airless will not be successful, because people will simply find ways to override its systems.

F

Cost remains a major obstacle. Some clients still assume that green buildings are always more expensive, especially when they see the price of solar panels, specialist glass or monitoring systems. Yet the calculation is changing. Passive design may reduce the size of heating and cooling equipment, while lower energy bills can save money over many years. Governments are also tightening building codes and offering incentives for efficient construction. In the long term, the more expensive option may be to build in the old way and then pay for high energy use, overheating and repairs.

G

The future of green architecture will probably be less spectacular than some early images suggested. Many successful buildings may look ordinary from the street. Their value will lie in careful orientation, durable materials, shade, ventilation, water reuse and repairable parts. Architects are also beginning to design buildings that can change use rather than being demolished when needs shift. This flexibility matters because the greenest building is often the one that lasts. If a structure can be adapted instead of replaced, the carbon already spent on making it is not wasted.

Questions 1-7

Do the following statements agree with the claims of the writer in Reading Passage 1?

In boxes 1-7 on your answer sheet, write

YES if the statement agrees with the claims of the writer
NO if the statement contradicts the claims of the writer
NOT GIVEN if it is impossible to say what the writer thinks about this

1 Green architecture is now becoming more relevant to ordinary construction.
2 Attractive appearance is no longer considered important in architecture.
3 Passive design can reduce energy demand before advanced technology is installed.
4 Engineered timber is always more environmentally friendly than recycled steel.
5 Some buildings reuse water from sinks and showers.
6 Most clients now believe green buildings are cheaper to construct than conventional buildings.
7 Adapting an existing building can be greener than replacing it.

Questions 8-13

Complete the summary below.

Choose ONE WORD ONLY from the passage for each answer.

Write your answers in boxes 8-13 on your answer sheet.

Designing greener buildings

Future green buildings may use passive design before expensive technology is added. Correct 8 .................... can reduce the need for artificial cooling, while 9 .................... can keep indoor temperatures stable. Architects are also choosing materials with lower embodied 10 .................... . Some buildings collect 11 .................... from roofs, and others treat greywater for reuse. Inside buildings, sensors may monitor air 12 .................... . Designers are also creating buildings that can change 13 .................... instead of being demolished.
rawAnswerKey:
Question 1: Green architecture is now becoming more relevant to ordinary construction.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: By 2026, it has moved much closer to the centre of ordinary construction.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะข้อความในโจทย์สอดคล้องกับ passage โดยตรง หลักฐานคือ “it has moved much closer to the centre of ordinary construction” ซึ่งหมายความว่า green architecture สำคัญต่อการก่อสร้างทั่วไปมากขึ้น หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “By 2026, it has moved much closer to the centre of ordinary construction.” เมื่อเทียบกับโจทย์ “Green architecture is now becoming more relevant to ordinary construction.” แล้วความหมายตรงกัน แม้คำบางคำถูก paraphrase ดังนั้นคำตอบคือ YES เพราะ passage ยืนยันข้อมูลเดียวกับโจทย์อย่างชัดเจน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: becoming more relevant = moved much closer to the centre; ordinary construction = ordinary construction; answer signal = YES

Question 2:Attractive appearance is no longer considered important in architecture.

Correct Answer: NO

Accepted Answers: NO

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: Architects therefore increasingly describe a good building not only as one that looks attractive, but as one that uses resources carefully

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NO เพราะโจทย์บอกว่า attractive appearance ไม่สำคัญแล้ว แต่ passage ใช้คำว่า not only as one that looks attractive แปลว่ายังสำคัญอยู่ เพียงแต่ต้องมีเรื่อง resources และ climate adaptation เพิ่มด้วย หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “Architects therefore increasingly describe a good building not only as one that looks attractive, but as one that uses resources carefully” เมื่อเทียบกับโจทย์ “The writer says that attractive appearance is no longer considered important in architecture.” แล้ว passage ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามกับข้อความในโจทย์ จึงไม่ใช่แค่ไม่มีข้อมูล แต่เป็นการขัดแย้งโดยตรง ดังนั้นคำตอบคือ NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: no longer important = contradicted by not only looks attractive; good building = good building; answer signal = NO

Question 3: Passive design can reduce energy demand before advanced technology is installed.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: In many climates, these passive design choices reduce energy demand before expensive technology is added.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะโจทย์ถอดความจาก passage โดยตรง passive design choices reduce energy demand และเกิดขึ้น before expensive technology is added หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “In many climates, these passive design choices reduce energy demand before expensive technology is added.” เมื่อเทียบกับโจทย์ “Passive design can reduce energy demand before advanced technology is installed.” แล้วความหมายตรงกัน แม้คำบางคำถูก paraphrase ดังนั้นคำตอบคือ YES เพราะ passage ยืนยันข้อมูลเดียวกับโจทย์อย่างชัดเจน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: advanced technology = expensive technology; installed = added; reduce energy demand = reduce energy demand; answer signal = YES

Question 4: Engineered timber is always more environmentally friendly than recycled steel.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: some architects are using engineered timber, recycled bricks, low-carbon concrete and reused steel from demolished buildings

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage กล่าวถึง engineered timber และ reused steel ว่าเป็นตัวเลือกวัสดุ แต่ไม่ได้เปรียบเทียบว่า timber ดีกว่า recycled steel เสมอหรือไม่ คำว่า always ไม่มีหลักฐานรองรับ หลักฐานที่เกี่ยวข้องคือ “some architects are using engineered timber, recycled bricks, low-carbon concrete and reused steel from demolished buildings” แต่เมื่อเทียบกับโจทย์ “Engineered timber is always more environmentally friendly than recycled steel.” แล้ว passage ไม่ได้ยืนยันรายละเอียดสำคัญของโจทย์อย่างครบถ้วน และไม่ได้ให้ข้อมูลตรงข้ามโดยตรง ดังนั้นต้องตอบ NOT GIVEN ไม่ใช่ TRUE/FALSE หรือ YES/NO

Paraphrased Vocabulary: engineered timber = engineered timber; recycled steel = reused steel; always more environmentally friendly = no comparison stated; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 5: Some buildings reuse water from sinks and showers.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: Others treat greywater from sinks and showers so that it can be used again.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะ passage บอกตรง ๆ ว่า greywater from sinks and showers can be used again ซึ่งตรงกับ reuse water from sinks and showers หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “Others treat greywater from sinks and showers so that it can be used again.” เมื่อเทียบกับโจทย์ “Some buildings reuse water from sinks and showers.” แล้วความหมายตรงกัน แม้คำบางคำถูก paraphrase ดังนั้นคำตอบคือ YES เพราะ passage ยืนยันข้อมูลเดียวกับโจทย์อย่างชัดเจน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: reuse = used again; water from sinks and showers = greywater from sinks and showers; answer signal = YES

Question 6: Most clients now believe green buildings are cheaper to construct than conventional buildings.

Correct Answer: NOT GIVEN

Accepted Answers: NOT GIVEN

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: Some clients still assume that green buildings are always more expensive

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ NOT GIVEN เพราะ passage บอกเพียงว่า some clients still assume green buildings are always more expensive แต่ไม่ได้บอกว่า most clients now believe green buildings are cheaper หรือไม่ ข้อมูลเรื่องสัดส่วน most และความเชื่อว่า cheaper ไม่มีหลักฐานพอ

Paraphrased Vocabulary: most clients = no majority information; cheaper to construct = no direct information; some clients assume more expensive = not enough to prove most believe cheaper; answer signal = NOT GIVEN

Question 7:Adapting an existing building can be greener than replacing it.

Correct Answer: YES

Accepted Answers: YES

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: If a structure can be adapted instead of replaced, the carbon already spent on making it is not wasted.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ YES เพราะ passage บอกว่าถ้าโครงสร้างถูก adapted แทน replaced จะไม่ waste carbon ที่ใช้สร้างไปแล้ว แสดงว่าการปรับใช้ของเดิมอาจ greener กว่าการแทนที่ หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “If a structure can be adapted instead of replaced, the carbon already spent on making it is not wasted.” เมื่อเทียบกับโจทย์ “The writer suggests that adapting an existing building can be greener than replacing it.” แล้วความหมายตรงกัน แม้คำบางคำถูก paraphrase ดังนั้นคำตอบคือ YES เพราะ passage ยืนยันข้อมูลเดียวกับโจทย์อย่างชัดเจน

Paraphrased Vocabulary: adapting an existing building = structure can be adapted; replacing = replaced; greener = carbon not wasted; answer signal = YES

Question 8: Correct .................... can reduce the need for artificial cooling, while insulation can keep indoor temperatures stable.

Correct Answer: orientation

Accepted Answers: orientation

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: A building's orientation may reduce the need for artificial cooling.

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ orientation เพราะ summary ถามว่า Correct ____ can reduce artificial cooling และ passage ระบุ “orientation may reduce the need for artificial cooling” ตรงตัว หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “A building's orientation may reduce the need for artificial cooling.” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ orientation เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: correct = a building's; reduce the need = reduce the need; artificial cooling = artificial cooling; answer signal = orientation

Question 9: Correct orientation can reduce the need for artificial cooling, while .................... can keep indoor temperatures stable.

Correct Answer: insulation

Accepted Answers: insulation

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: insulation can keep indoor temperatures stable

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ insulation เพราะ passage ระบุว่า insulation can keep indoor temperatures stable ซึ่งตรงกับช่องว่างใน summary โดยตรง หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “insulation can keep indoor temperatures stable” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ insulation เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: keep = keep; indoor temperatures stable = indoor temperatures stable; answer signal = insulation

Question 10: Architects are also choosing materials with lower embodied .................... .

Correct Answer: carbon

Accepted Answers: carbon

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: choosing materials with lower embodied carbon

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ carbon เพราะ summary ถาม lower embodied ____ และ passage ใช้วลี “lower embodied carbon” ตรงตัว คำนี้เป็นคำเดียวตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “choosing materials with lower embodied carbon” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ carbon เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: choosing materials = choosing materials; lower embodied carbon = lower embodied carbon; answer signal = carbon

Question 11: Some buildings collect .................... from roofs, and others treat greywater for reuse.

Correct Answer: rainwater

Accepted Answers: rainwater

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: Some new buildings collect rainwater from roofs

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ rainwater เพราะ summary บอกว่า collect ____ from roofs และ passage ระบุ “collect rainwater from roofs” โดยตรง หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “Some new buildings collect rainwater from roofs” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ rainwater เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: some buildings = some new buildings; collect from roofs = collect from roofs; answer signal = rainwater

Question 12: Inside buildings, sensors may monitor air .................... .

Correct Answer: quality

Accepted Answers: quality

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: sensors to monitor temperature, humidity and air quality

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ quality เพราะ summary ถาม air ____ และ passage ระบุ “air quality” ในรายการที่ sensors monitor จึงตอบ quality หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “sensors to monitor temperature, humidity and air quality” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ quality เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: inside buildings = some offices; monitor = monitor; air quality = air quality; answer signal = quality

Question 13: Designers are also creating buildings that can change .................... instead of being demolished.

Correct Answer: use

Accepted Answers: use

Answer Group: IELTS Academic Reading March 2026 Passage 1 - The Future of Green Architecture

Exact Portion: design buildings that can change use rather than being demolished

Short Thai Explanation: คำตอบคือ use เพราะ summary บอกว่า buildings can change ____ instead of being demolished และ passage ระบุ “change use rather than being demolished” ตรงตัว หลักฐานสำคัญคือ “design buildings that can change use rather than being demolished” คำที่ต้องเติมคือ use เพราะเป็นคำจาก passage โดยตรง เข้ากับไวยากรณ์ของประโยคใน summary และตรงตามคำสั่ง ONE WORD ONLY จึงไม่ควรใช้คำพ้องหรือเพิ่มคำอื่น

Paraphrased Vocabulary: instead of = rather than; demolished = demolished; answer signal = use
